items.title: Public sex offenders registry still under discussion - Nancy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Relevant ministries are still in discussions on establishing a public sex offenders registry, says Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri. She said setting up the registry is a complex task involving various legal and administrative hurdles. Nancy was responding to a question on whether the ministry would push for a public registry of sexual offenders, following an increase in cases of child sexual abuse, including online offences.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ rush begins at Kuala Lumpur transport hubs as travellers try to beat Aidiladha crowds
items.summary: A Bernama survey found increasing crowds at key terminals, including Terminal Bersepadu Gombak (TBG), Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS) and KL Sentral, as passengers began making early travel preparations to avoid last-minute congestion. A TBG representative, Mohd Raimi Jusoh, said ticket sales for the festive season opened two months ago to give passengers more time to plan their journeys. He said daily ticket sales at TBG, which usually average about 1,000, have surged to around 7,000 during the long holiday and Aidiladha period.

items.title: 'Geng Hadi' busted over motorcycle thefts in Sentul
items.summary: Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 15 and 33, were believed to have stolen motorcycles for spare parts. "Following the report, the Sentul district police headquarters' D4 Criminal Investigation Division conducted intelligence gathering and successfully arrested the six suspects while also seizing two motorcycles," he told reporters today. Jasni said police believed the arrest of all six suspects, including the syndicate mastermind, had helped solve at least eight motorcycle theft cases in the Sentul district.

items.title: Health Ministry to expand treatment scope of private clinics to empower GPs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MoH) plans to expand the scope of treatments at private clinics as part of measures to improve existing policies to empower general practitioners (GP). The MoH said the measures include allowing services such as ultrasound and Occupational Health Doctor (OHD) services offered effectively and safely. "The MoH is reviewing policy improvements by drafting implementation directives for specific service scopes at private clinics," the ministry said.

items.title: No other trigger linked to Zara Qairina’s death besides dorm interrogation, inquest told
items.summary: Abdul Fikry: Without the confrontation there is a possibility Zara may have committed suicide due to other triggers or destabilising factors, correct? Abdul Fikry: Before the confrontation, Zara had exhibited predominant suicide risk factors? Dr Chua: Correct.

items.title: Clothing brand founder, six others fined over Bukit Bintang flare incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Seven men, including a local clothing brand founder, pleaded guilty in the magistrates' courts here to multiple offences linked to a viral publicity stunt involving flares and reckless driving in Bukit Bintang last weekend. Mohamad Azhar Mohamed Idris, 31, was fined RM4,500, in default six months' jail, for stopping a vehicle in a dangerous manner at a traffic light junction along Jalan Sultan Ismail and Jalan Bukit Bintang at 2.07am on May 18. Five of the accused, namely, Amir Adzim Shahrul Nizam, 25; Khairul Hamizan Zainudin, 28; Muhammad Izz Mifzal Mohd Noordzlie, 22; Muhammad Ariff Aiman Mohamed Nasir, 23; and Muhammad Ikhwan Daniel Azman, 24, pleaded guilty before magistrate Aina Azahra Arifin to committing public nuisance at Shibuya Junction in Bukit Bintang at about 2am on May 18.

items.title: Three Myanmar nationals remanded over compatriot’s death in Kepong
items.summary: Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 30 and 40, were arrested last Monday. “Initial investigations found that the incident was believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding that led to a fight between the victim and all the suspects, who were intoxicated, resulting in the victim’s death,” he said at a press conference at the Sentul district police headquarters today. In another development, Jasni said police detained four individuals — one man and three women — to assist in investigations into a fight in Sentul believed to have been caused by personal disputes and debt issues.

items.title: Myanmar man beaten to death after drunken dispute in Kepong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A misunderstanding fuelled by alcohol among three Myanmar citizens ended in tragedy when one of them was beaten to death beneath the Metro Prima Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) station bridge in Kepong on Sunday. Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said police received a report from a member of the public at 1.40pm regarding an unconscious 39-year-old man at the location. Following the report, a team from the Sentul district police headquarters criminal investigation division launched an operation around Kepong at about 12.30pm on Monday.

items.title: Farm Fresh posts lower Q4 earnings, may hike select Malaysia and Singapore product prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd's net profit slightly declined to RM27.9 million in the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2024 (Q4 FY26) from RM28.35 million a year ago. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia the company said the decline was due to higher distribution costs resulting from increased school milk sales, accompanied with the freight costs arising from exports to Cambodia and Philippines. For the full financial year, Farm Fresh's earnings climbed to RM129.61 million from RM106.4 million a year ago while revenue rose to RM1.12 billion from RM981.18 million.

items.title: PM: IWK award reflects Malaysia's sustainability commitment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the international recognition received by Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) reflects the country's commitment to strengthening sustainable water and sanitation management. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said efforts to reinforce this are in line with the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda (SDGs). The award recognises the company's submission, 'Solar-powered Sanitation: No Upfront Cost, Cuts Emissions', which demonstrates how the use of renewable energy can enhance wastewater treatment operational performance, drive sustainable infrastructure development and strengthen financial resilience.

items.title: Alliance Bank logs in 10.1pct higher net profit to RM826.53mil in FY26
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd's net profit rose 10.1 per cent to RM826.53 million in the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM750.53 million. The bank's revenue grew 8.65 per cent to RM2.47 billion from RM2.27 billion. NOII jumped 42 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM459.2 million.

items.title: Zara always slept in class, Coroner's court told
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir often slept in class, according to her classmates, dormmates and teacher. When replying to lawyer Datuk Rizwandean M.Borhan, Dr Chua said he is aware of a school warden's testimony that Zara Qairina had slept less in the class after first semester examination. Rizwandean: Yesterday, I mentioned about (Zara Qairina's) disappointment over the Badar issue (prefect) and chronic insufficient sleep.

items.title: Food company fined RM21,000 for falsifying halal logo
items.summary: Wong was found to have falsely described seven "Pemancing" peanut product packages as halal, in contravention of Paragraph 4(1) of the Trade Descriptions (Certification and Marking of Halal) Order 2011. The offence was committed at the company's premises in Jalan Besar Sungai Siput (U), Sungai Siput, at 11am on Nov 3, 2025. The court was told the factory raided during the operation did not possess Malaysian halal certification, although the same company held certification for another factory in Jalan Lintang, Sungai Siput.

items.title: MMEA detains over 4,000 undocumented migrants in first four months of 2026
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 22 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained 4,107 illegal immigrants and 316 smugglers nationwide in the first four months of this year. MMEA deputy director-general of Operations Vice Admiral Mohd Zawawi Abdullah said migrant smuggling syndicates remain active and exploit the country’s waters due to Malaysia’s strategic position in the region. Mohd Zawawi said MMEA has opened eight investigation papers related to migrant smuggling cases involving 19 smugglers and 233 illegal immigrants.

items.title: Eighteen-year-old alleged gang leader among six arrested in Sentul motorcycle theft crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Police have crippled a motorcycle theft syndicate known as ‘Geng Hadi’ following the arrest of six local men, including its 18-year-old leader, in a series of raids in Kepong last Saturday, leading to the resolution of eight theft cases in Sentul involving estimated losses of RM20,000. Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 15 and 33, were detained during an operation conducted by the D4 Branch of the Criminal Investigation Division at the Sentul district police headquarters (IPD). He said during the raids, police seized two motorcycles, five incomplete motorcycle frames, one set of motorcycle components and two mobile phones.

items.title: Malaysia's involvement in BRICS to strengthen global economic position
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Malaysia's participation in BRICS is seen as having the potential to strengthen the country's position in global economic competition, as well as reduce dependence on the traditional financial system dominated by major powers, said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Mohamad, who is also Rembau MP and Rantau assemblyman, said Malaysia is now expanding the use of local currencies in trade with neighbouring countries, including ASEAN members, to reduce reliance on third-party currencies. "Malaysia is a trading nation.

items.title: Mohamad Hasan says Brics offers Malaysia stronger footing in global economy
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 22 — Malaysia’s participation in Brics is seen as having the potential to strengthen the country’s position in global economic competition, as well as reduce dependence on the traditional financial system dominated by major powers, said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Mohamad, who is also Rembau MP and Rantau assemblyman, said Malaysia is now expanding the use of local currencies in trade with neighbouring countries, including Asean members, to reduce reliance on third-party currencies. “Malaysia is a trading nation.

items.title: Govt strives to secure energy supply security until December
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government is working to ensure that the country's energy supply security can be extended until December, following the global supply crisis triggered by conflicts in the Middle East. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the nation's energy supply security is currently projected to be sufficient until July, with ongoing efforts to secure additional resources from abroad. "At present, our energy supply security will last until July.

items.title: Mother might not know Zara Qairina's perception of her, expert says
items.summary: Lawyer Joan Goh questioned consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung whether Zara Qairina had written in her diary about being scolded over handphone usage, where the teen questioned why her mother had beaten her over it. Dr Chua: It is possible. Dr Chua: Yes.

items.title: France may consider windfall tax on sectors buoyed by Iran crisis, finance minister says
items.summary: PARIS: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said on Friday the government may consider imposing a windfall tax on companies amid the surge in energy prices during the war in Iran. "If there are particular companies that have made an exceptional amount, maybe we will impose additional taxes, I'll say it that way, but this debate will take place in the fall," Lescure told Sud Radio. Several opposition French politicians have advocated for additional so-called windfall taxes on oil companies such as TotalEnergies since the war in Iran started in late February.

items.title: Johor special assembly session on June 24 remains unconfirmed
items.summary: Earlier, Samsolbari, who is also Ayer Hitam Umno division chief, joined 25 other division chiefs for a committee meeting at the Johor Umno liaison office here. The meeting was chaired by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is the state Umno liaison chairman and Simpang Renggam Umno division chief. Also present were Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan and state Umno information chief Md Israk Abdullah.

items.title: Prisons Dept: Inmates may receive family visits on May 29–30 for Aidiladha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Families of inmates at all prison institutions, including Moral Rehabilitation Centres, Special Rehabilitation Centres, Special Detention Facilities and Henry Gurney Schools, are allowed to visit them for the Aidiladha celebration on the third and fourth days of the festive period. Instead, these may be purchased at the prison canteen,” it said. Families seeking further information may contact the relevant institutions.

items.title: Anwar visits HKL, prays for patients' recovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a visit to Hospital Kuala Lumpur (HKL) to observe its operations and meet patients receiving treatment at the facility. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he also took the opportunity to greet patients during the visit. The prime minister also expressed appreciation to HKL healthcare workers for their continued dedication and commitment in caring for the public.

items.title: Authorities investigating viral claims of newborn thrown from 10th floor of PPR flat
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Authorities are investigating a viral claim involving a newborn baby girl who allegedly died after being thrown from the 10th floor of a People's Housing Project (PPR) flat here. Johor Women, Family and Community Development Committee chairman Khairin Nisa Ismail said the incident, believed to have occurred several days ago, has sparked widespread public concern. Meanwhile, a police spokesman confirmed that the incident is currently under investigation.

items.title: MCMC unveils regulatory codes under Online Safety Act to protect children online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) today unveiled two regulatory codes under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) to better protect minors from harmful online content. "The codes set clear expectations for service providers to take greater responsibility in addressing online harms within their services, particularly in safeguarding children and vulnerable users. "The implementation approach under the codes remains outcome-based, allowing service providers the flexibility to adopt appropriate solutions that meet safety, privacy and legal requirements."

items.title: Dissolution of Johor state assembly is Sultan Ibrahim’s absolute prerogative, says Tok Mat
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 22 — The dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for a state election is the absolute prerogative of the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, according to Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. He said the party was confident that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi would advise Sultan Ibrahim, who is the Sultan of Johor, on the appropriate timing for the state election. On May 16, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor BN chairman, announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election.

items.title: High Court rejects application to freeze RM548mil assets linked to Daim's wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has rejected an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to secure an access restriction order against RM548 million worth of assets allegedly linked to Toh Puan Na'imah Abdul Khalid, the wife of the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin, her son, and seven other parties. The offshore assets comprise investment funds totalling GBP21 million (about RM121 million) and US$99 million (about RM423 million) held in Singapore. The application was filed under Section 53 of Amlatfpuaa 2001 following investigations conducted under Section 113 of the Income Tax Act and Section 4(1) of Amlatfpuaa.

items.title: Man found dead in drain died of heart disease, no foul play suspected
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have ruled out foul play in the death of a man whose body was found in a drain beside a shop at Jalan Kedah here yesterday. Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said a post-mortem conducted at the Penang Hospital Forensic Department today found that the cause of death was Ischemic Heart Disease. "The post-mortem results showed that the victim died due to blocked heart blood vessels (Ischemic Heart Disease).

items.title: RM20,000 debt, love dispute sparked viral Sentul Raya brawl
items.summary: Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said police have so far arrested four local suspects comprising two men and two women. He said investigations revealed the altercation stemmed from personal issues and unpaid debts among individuals who knew each other. All four suspects have been remanded for two days beginning yesterday and the remand ends today.

items.title: Minister urges firm action over alleged sexual grooming of 14‑year‑old boy by female teacher
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM) has called for strict action over a case involving a female teacher alleged to have engaged in sexual grooming and misconduct involving a 14-year-old male student. Its Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the case must not be taken lightly and urged the relevant agencies to take firm and immediate action. The ministry under which she is employed should also take firm action,” she said.

items.title: Man charged with Pulau Gaya murder after body found floating near Kampung Lobong
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 22 — A 48-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a man whose body was found floating in waters off Kampung Lobong, Pulau Gaya, earlier this month. No plea was recorded from Ali Daham when he appeared before Magistrate Marilyn Kelvin, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Ali was accused of murdering Shazwan Salim at a water village house in Kampung Pondo, Pulau Gaya, at about 9am on May 4.

items.title: Hostel interrogation one of the main factors which led to Zara's suicide, expert says
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The interrogation of Zara Qairina Mahathir by her seniors was one of the main factors that led to the 13-year-old found lying unconscious outside her dormitory on July 16 last year, an expert told the Coroner's court. Consultant Forensic Psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that the interrogation took place when Zara Qairina was called to A-3-6 dormitory between 9pm and midnight on July 15. "The deceased had multiple predisposing suicide risk factors.

items.title: Sabah Fire Dept: 72‑year‑old woman found dead after blaze destroys Papar home
items.summary: PAPAR, May 22 — A 72-year-old woman died after a two-storey semi-permanent house caught fire at Kampung Seri Tanjung, Pengalat Kecil, here, this morning. According to a Sabah Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson, a team of seven personnel led by Senior Fire Officer Dahlan Jainudin rushed to the scene after receiving a distress call at 6.29am and arrived at 6.35am at the location, about 4km from the fire station. He said the team later found the unconscious elderly woman in the bathroom area at 7.57am and she was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel, believed to have died from breathing difficulties during the fire.

items.title: Jamal Yunos issues public apology to Khairy, settles defamation suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The defamation suit filed by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin against Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos was resolved today after the Sungai Besar Umno division chief issued a public apology before the High Court here. Khairy initiated the legal action on May 24, 2023, following defamatory remarks made during a media statement at the 2022 Umno General Assembly on Jan 14 that year. "Accordingly, I hereby retract fully all defamatory statements I issued against Khairy during the media conference and in the said publication," he said.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolution absolute prerogative of the King: Mohamad Hasan
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for a snap state election is the absolute prerogative of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, says Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. He said the coalition remains confident that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi will advise Sultan Ibrahim, who is the Sultan of Johor, on the appropriate timing for the state polls. On May 16, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor BN chairman, announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election.

items.title: MSM names Mazlan Kamaruddin as new chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MSM Malaysia Holdings Bhd has appointed Datuk Mazlan Kamaruddin as its chairman effective immediately. He also previously worked at Malayan Banking Bhd, then known as United Malayan Banking Corp Bhd, in corporate planning and investments between 1985 and 1990. Mazlan currently sits on the boards of several companies within the Naza Group ecosystem including Naza TTDI Sdn Bhd, Naza Automotive Holdings Sdn Bhd and Naza Motor Trading Sdn Bhd as an independent non-executive director.

items.title: Family members will find it difficult to accept Zara committed suicide, court hears
items.summary: Dr Chua: Agree. Dr Chua: Agree. Dr Chua: Agree.

items.title: Critical Holdings unit bags RM152.6mil contract from "renowned American MNC'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Critical Holdings Bhd's unit Critical Engineering Solutions Sdn Bhd has secured a RM152.6 million engineering, procurement and construction contract for cleanroom and utilities works at Kulim Hi-Tech Park in Kedah. The contract covers the construction of a new cleanroom architecture associated with mechanical, electrical and process utilities services at the industrial park. Critical Holdings said the contract is expected to contribute to the company's earnings and net assets for the financial year ending June 30, 2027.

items.title: Jamal Yunos issues public apology, retracts corruption allegations against Khairy in court settlement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The defamation suit filed by former Health Minister Khairy Jamaluddin against Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos was resolved today after the Sungai Besar Umno Division chief issued a public apology before the High Court here. Khairy initiated the suit on May 24, 2023, arising from a media statement allegedly containing defamatory remarks made during the 2022 Umno General Assembly on January 14 of that year. “Accordingly, I hereby retract fully all defamatory statements I issued against Khairy during the media conference and in the said publication,” he said.

items.title: MARC Ratings affirms stable outlook on CIMB, CIMB Islamic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MARC Ratings has affirmed a stable outlook on its ratings for CIMB Group Bhd and CIMB Islamic Bank Bhd. The agency affirmed its corporate credit ratings on CIMB at AA+/MARC-1 and the AA rating on the bank's RM10 billion Basel III–compliant Tier 2 subordinated debt programme. "The group's funding profile is sound, supported by a stable funding ratio of 83.8 per cent and a healthy current and savings account (CASA) ratio of 46.2 per cent in 2025.

items.title: Hibiscus Petroleum swings back to profit in Q3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd returned to the black in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26), supported by higher oil prices. The company posted a net profit of RM80.1 million, reversing from a net loss of RM116 million a year earlier, lifted by a 12 per cent quarter-on-quarter rise in its average realised oil and condensate price to US$76.7 per barrel. However, revenue slipped 9.6 per cent to RM517.8 million from RM572.8 million a year ago, according to a bourse filing.

items.title: Pas reviews Bersatu's conduct
items.summary: MARANG: Pas has not set a deadline for Bersatu to clarify its actions, which allegedly violate the spirit of camaraderie within Perikatan Nasional (PN). Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, however, said the party's Syura Ulama Council and election department have been tasked with examining every negative action taken by Bersatu previously. Hadi, who is also the Marang member of parliament, said the recent moves by Pas would not hinder PN's preparations for any upcoming elections.

items.title: Malaysia to tighten child online protection following surge in CSAM‑linked offences, says Nancy Shukri
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — The government is tightening online protection for children, including through amendments under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) 2025, following a rise in digital sexual crimes involving minors. “We are amending the ONSA 2025 to strengthen online safety protection, particularly for children. She said the move follows growing concern over online child sexual exploitation, including the collection of child sexual abuse materials.

items.title: Vehicles damaged by debris on dark Bandar Sierra road in Kota Kinabalu; minister presses for swift action
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 22 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud has raised safety concerns over poor lighting along a stretch of road near Bandar Sierra after several vehicles were reportedly damaged by an object believed to have fallen from a heavy vehicle. During the discussion, a JKR official said permanent street lights at the location had already been installed and that the matter was currently in the process of obtaining approval for electricity supply connection. The official said efforts would be made to find an immediate electricity source to enable the lights to be switched on temporarily while awaiting full approval, adding that damaged lights would also be repaired.

items.title: Anwar visits HKL, meets patients and thanks healthcare workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today visited the Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) to observe its operations while meeting patients as well as members of the public receiving treatment at the hospital. “I took the opportunity to observe operations at the HKL today while greeting and checking on members of the public receiving treatment. Anwar also expressed appreciation to HKL healthcare workers for their dedication and sacrifices in delivering services to the people.

items.title: Fadillah: Govt assures 85pc of consumers to remain protected from electricity tariff hikes
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA), said domestic consumers using up to 600 kilowatt-hours (kWh) of electricity per month would not be affected, although minor adjustments may apply to those exceeding the threshold. Fadillah said any anticipated increase would not involve a blanket electricity tariff hike, but would instead stem from global supply disruptions that have driven up logistics and supply chain costs. He said consumers using more than 600kWh per month might face slight adjustments.

items.title: Prosecution fails in bid to freeze RM544m allegedly held by Na’imah, her son and seven others in Singapore
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The High Court today dismissed the prosecution’s bid for a prohibition order on approximately RM544 million allegedly owned by Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, her son, and seven others in Singapore bank accounts. They claimed that the assets in question were investment funds totalling £21 million (approximately RM121 million) and USD99 million (approximately RM423 million) held in Singapore. The application was filed under Section 53 of the AMLA Act 2001, arising from investigations conducted under Section 113 of the Income Tax Act and Section 4(1) of the AMLA Act 2001.

items.title: Alleged RM20,000 debt, love feud behind viral Sentul street brawl, say cops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — A dispute over a RM20,000 debt and a relationship conflict are believed to have sparked a viral brawl in the Sentul Raya area on Wednesday afternoon. According to Harian Metro , Sentul deputy district police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said police have so far arrested four local suspects, comprising two men and two women. All the suspects have been remanded for two days from yesterday.

items.title: Think City report: Stress, sleepless nights and violent crime still plague Klang Valley PPR residents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — A majority of residents living in public housing continue to struggle with mental health issues more than three years after the Covid-19 pandemic subsided, according to a recent report by Think City. Three out of four residents surveyed across 10 People’s Housing Projects (PPRs) in the Klang Valley said their stress levels had either not improved or had worsened since the pandemic. Suicide rates across the surveyed PPRs — estimated at about 20 cases per 100,000 people annually, excluding attempts — were more than three times higher than the national average of about six cases per 100,000 people.

items.title: Anwar: IWK’s win at Global Water Awards reflects Malaysia’s commitment to sustainable water and sanitation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) Sdn Bhd for receiving recognition at the Global Water Awards 2026 in Madrid recently, saying the achievement reflects Malaysia’s strong commitment to strengthening sustainable water and sanitation management. Last Tuesday, the national sewerage company was named winner of the Sustainable Development Goal 6 (SDG 6) Award at the Global Water Awards, held in conjunction with the Global Water Summit aimed at recognising excellence, leadership in sustainability and technological innovation in the global water industry. The award, presented by Global Water Intelligence, recognised IWK’s entry titled Solar-powered Sanitation: No Upfront Cost, Cuts Emissions, which showcased how the use of renewable energy can strengthen the operational performance of sewage treatment, while driving sustainable infrastructure development and enhancing financial resilience.

items.title: Extra Langkawi ferry trips added as school holiday and Aidiladha bookings top 10,000 passengers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Ferry services to Langkawi from Kuala Kedah and Kuala Perlis will increase from tomorrow in anticipation of higher passenger traffic during the school holidays and Aidiladha celebrations. According to Berita Harian, Konsortium Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd operations manager Norhafiz Abdul Wahid said one additional daily trip from both ferry terminals has been scheduled to accommodate rising demand from domestic and international tourists. He said ferry operations from Kuala Kedah and Kuala Perlis had previously been reduced to three trips daily since March 25 following the increase in diesel prices.

items.title: Police obtain six-day remand for Johor teacher in sexual misconduct probe
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 22 — Police have obtained a six-day remand order starting today against a male teacher following his arrest in connection with a sexual misconduct case involving a primary school pupil in Seri Alam, Pasir Gudang. Seri Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Sohaimi Ishak said the 28-year-old suspect will be remanded until May 27 to assist in investigations. Earlier, police confirmed the arrest of the suspect at 10.30pm last night following allegations of sexual misconduct involving a 12-year-old primary schoolgirl.

items.title: Saarani leads Perak mission to Taopu in Shanghai to examine smart city tech and command‑centre systems
items.summary: IPOH, May 22 — The Perak state government is advancing its smart city agenda with a technical visit to Taopu Smart City in Shanghai, China, to study integrated technology and urban management systems that could be applied at home. The Ipoh City Council (MBI) said in a statement that the Perak delegation was led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, accompanied by Ipoh Mayor Zamakhshari Hanipah. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad led the Perak delegation to Taopu Smart City in Shanghai, accompanied by Ipoh Mayor Zamakhshari Hanipah.

items.title: Penang to tackle rising stray cat numbers with proposed trap-neuter-release programme
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 22 — The Penang Island City Council (MBPP) is planning to introduce a trap-neuter-release (TNR) programme to curb the growing stray cat population on the island following the success of a similar initiative for stray dogs. MBPP Mayor Datuk A. Rajendran said the move is necessary as the number of stray cats has been increasing, especially around wet markets and public eateries. He said another way to combat the abandonment of pets, both cats and dogs, which has contributed to the rise in stray animals, is through the introduction of mandatory microchipping for pets.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: SuRIA Home to offer up to RM3,000 in rebates to boost residential rooftop solar adoption
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, said that RM150 million has been allocated for the SuRIA Home solar rebate initiative. He said that under the initiative, domestic users who install solar systems under the Solar Accelerated Transition Action Programme (Solar ATAP) and meet the eligibility criteria can receive a rebate of RM600 for every 1kWac of solar installation, up to a maximum of RM3,000, equivalent to 5kWac. Fadillah said the SuRIA Home programme is open to solar installations under the Solar ATAP programme that commence operations by Dec 31, 2026, or until the 250MW quota is fully taken up, whichever comes first.

items.title: Penang’s zero-kill approach sees 8,484 stray dogs neutered since 2018
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 22 — Penang Island has not put down any stray dogs since adopting a Trap-Neuter-Release (TNR) programme in 2018, with 8,484 dogs neutered under the initiative. Penang Island City Council (MBPP) Mayor Datuk A. Rajendran said the TNR programme, implemented on March 1, 2018, was the result of a collaboration with the Independent Aid for Protection and Welfare of Animals (IAPWA) Penang. “As of May 17, a total of 8,484 dogs have been successfully neutered under this programme,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia working to secure energy supply security until December, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — The government is working to ensure the country’s energy supply security can be extended until December amid the global supply crisis that has disrupted economic chains and increased energy costs, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said the country’s energy supply is currently expected to be sufficient until July, and efforts are being intensified to extend this, including sourcing additional supplies from abroad. “At present, insya-Allah, our energy supply security will last until July.

items.title: Amirudin alleges sabotage after boat for Gaza aid mission sinks before departure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari today claimed that a boat he was supposed to use to join the final leg of a humanitarian mission to Gaza was sabotaged and sunk before departure. Amirudin, who is also leading the Malaysian Emergency Aid Mission to Gaza, said the vessel was believed to have been deliberately targeted after it was identified by parties linked to the Israeli regime. Despite the incident, Amirudin said the mission would continue efforts to draw global attention to the humanitarian crisis in Gaza.

items.title: Johor Education Department calls for thorough probe into teacher involved in sexual misconduct
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 22 — The Johor Education Department has assured the public that it will not compromise on cases of sexual misconduct in educational institutions under its administration, following the latest case involving a teacher in the state. In a statement, the department confirmed that several police reports have been lodged, including by the school, resulting in the teacher involved being remanded by police to assist in investigations. The Johor Education Department also advised members of the public who have experienced such cases to lodge police reports.

items.title: Two remanded over deadly gas cylinder blast at Sungai Petani expo site
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Two men were remanded today to assist investigations into a gas cylinder explosion at an expo site near Dataran Jam Besar in Sungai Petani yesterday. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the two men, aged 38 and 18, were believed to be linked to the incident and the sale of children’s balloons at the expo site. In the incident, one man was killed while three others were injured after a gas cylinder suddenly exploded.
